answer:
The probability of carrying two specific alleles, each with a frequency of 0.2, depends on whether the alleles are at the same locus (homozygous) or different loci. If they are at the same locus, the probability is \(0.2 \times 0.2 = 0.04\). If they are at different loci, the probability is \(2 \times 0.2 \times 0.2 = 0.08\) for heterozygous at both loci, but the question implies a single genotype with two specific alleles, so the most straightforward interpretation is \(0.2 \times 0.2 = 0.04\).

\boxed{H}
